deep_dive.title: ZONOS2 Technical Report
deep_dive.abstract: We present ZONOS2 8B, our latest TTS model, which achieves state-of-the-art naturalness, prosody, and voice cloning fidelity. We improve upon Zonos-v0.1 across scale, data, and training recipe. We scale the model from 1.6B to 8B total parameters (900M active) with a novel mixture-of-experts (MoE) backbone, improving inference latency and throughput. We expand our training corpus from 200K to over 6M hours using a new data processing pipeline, and we simplify our post-training and conditioning recipes to improve naturalness and voice cloning fidelity. We evaluate ZONOS2 8B on quality, speaker similarity, WER, and ZTTS1-Eval, our novel TTS benchmark, where it performs competitively with state-of-the-art systems while maintaining good streaming latency. We release our model weights and example inference code under an Apache 2.0 license on GitHub and Hugging Face.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: State-of-the-art multilingual TTS model with MoE architecture and large-scale training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具有MoE架构和大规模训练的最先进多语言TTS模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具有MoE架构和大规模训练的最先进多语言TTS模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具有MoE架构和大规模训练的最先进多语言TTS模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ZONOS2 8B is a state-of-the-art TTS model with MoE backbone, trained on 6M hours of data, achieving top-tier naturalness, prosody, and voice cloning. It includes a new benchmark and released weights.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出ZONOS2 8B TTS模型，采用混合专家（MoE）架构，参数量1.6B扩展到8B（激活900M），训练数据从20万小时增至600万小时。在自然度、韵律和声音克隆保真度上达到最先进水平，并发布了新基准ZTTS1-Eval。该模型在流式延迟下性能优越，推动了多语言语音合成技术的发展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出ZONOS2 8B TTS模型，采用混合专家（MoE）架构，参数量1.6B扩展到8B（激活900M），训练数据从20万小时增至600万小时。在自然度、韵律和声音克隆保真度上达到最先进水平，并发布了新基准ZTTS1-Eval。该模型在流式延迟下性能优越，推动了多语言语音合成技术的发展。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: multilingual speech synthesis models and techniques

deep_dive.title: Does Translation-Enhanced Speech Encoder Pre-training Affect Speech LLMs?
deep_dive.abstract: Connecting a pre-trained speech encoder to a Large Language Model (LLM) is the standard architecture for building Speech LLMs. However, a structural misalignment exists between the encoder and the LLM. Unlike encoders based on automatic speech recognition, which often produce representations in separate language-specific spaces, LLMs operate within a unified language-agnostic space. A mechanism is required to align the encoder's language-specific representations with the LLM's shared space. We argue that speech translation provides a principled way to achieve this. Unlike monolingual transcription, translation requires the model to bridge different languages and learn language-agnostic representations. We experimentally evaluate the impact of incorporating translation objectives into speech encoder pre-training. Our results demonstrate that translation-enhanced pre-training improves cross-modal integration and leads to superior performance across downstream Speech LLM tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: translation-enhanced speech encoder pre-training for cross-lingual alignment in speech LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 翻译增强的语音编码器预训练，用于语音大模型的跨语言对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 翻译增强的语音编码器预训练，用于语音大模型的跨语言对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 翻译增强的语音编码器预训练，用于语音大模型的跨语言对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Investigates whether using speech translation during encoder pre-training improves cross-lingual alignment between speech encoder and LLM, showing benefits over monolingual transcription.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 语音大模型中编码器与LLM存在结构不对齐：编码器输出语言特定表示，而LLM需要语言无关空间。该文论证语音翻译预训练能桥接这种差异，因为翻译要求跨语言桥接。实验表明，翻译增强预训练比单语转录更能提升语音LLM的跨语言性能，为构建多语言语音系统提供了有效策略。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 语音大模型中编码器与LLM存在结构不对齐：编码器输出语言特定表示，而LLM需要语言无关空间。该文论证语音翻译预训练能桥接这种差异，因为翻译要求跨语言桥接。实验表明，翻译增强预训练比单语转录更能提升语音LLM的跨语言性能，为构建多语言语音系统提供了有效策略。

deep_dive.title: Probing in the Wild: A Case Study of Self-Supervised Speech Representations on Mandarin Sub-dialects with Unsupervised Articulatory Analysis
deep_dive.abstract: While self-supervised speech models have achieved strong performance across speech tasks, relatively little is known about how their internal phonetic representations behave under fine-grained dialect variation. Existing probing studies typically rely on curated corpora with manual phonetic annotations, limiting their applicability to naturally occurring dialect speech. We present a case study of articulatory feature representations in a Mandarin self-supervised speech model using an entirely unlabeled probing pipeline. Phone sequences are generated using a language-agnostic universal phone recognizer and mapped to articulatory feature vectors, enabling frame-level probing without manual annotation. Our results reveal a structured pattern in articulatory feature decodability across Mandarin sub-dialects. Acoustically salient features such as labiality and stridency remain comparatively stable, whereas features associated with finer spectral distinctions exhibit larger dialect-dependent variation. This variation is driven primarily by elevated decodability for Beijing speech relative to other Mandarin sub-dialects. Layer-wise analyses further show distinct representational dynamics for these feature groups. These findings suggest that language-agnostic articulatory probing can be applied to real-world dialect corpora and that dialect sensitivity in self-supervised speech representations is unevenly distributed across articulatory dimensions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised speech representations on Mandarin sub-dialects with unsupervised articulatory analysis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 普通话次方言的自监督语音表示与无监督发音分析
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 普通话次方言的自监督语音表示与无监督发音分析
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 普通话次方言的自监督语音表示与无监督发音分析
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Probes self-supervised speech model on Mandarin dialects using unlabeled articulatory analysis, revealing how internal representations handle fine-grained dialect variation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自监督语音模型在细粒度方言变化下的内部语音表示行为尚不明确。该文以普通话次方言为例，提出完全无标签的探针流程：使用通用音素识别器生成音素序列，映射为发音特征向量，实现帧级探针。实验揭示了模型在处理方言变异时的模式，为方言语音识别提供了见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 自监督语音模型在细粒度方言变化下的内部语音表示行为尚不明确。该文以普通话次方言为例，提出完全无标签的探针流程：使用通用音素识别器生成音素序列，映射为发音特征向量，实现帧级探针。实验揭示了模型在处理方言变异时的模式，为方言语音识别提供了见解。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: processing and recognition of dialectal speech

quick_skim.title: BranchShine: Compact Raw-Audio-to-IPA Transcription with a RoPE E-Branchformer Encoder
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-to-IPA transcription is useful when the desired output is pronunciation rather than orthographic text, but competitive multilingual systems are often large and evaluation is sensitive to normalization choices. This paper presents BranchShine, a 33M-parameter raw-audio CTC recognizer with a lightweight convolutional front end and a 19-block RoPE E-Branchformer encoder. We find that BranchShine provides a compact and competitive operating point for IPA transcription under matched normalization and scoring. On a 16,660-utterance multilingual test set covering 41 language labels, BranchShine obtains 9.19% whitespace-insensitive IPA character error rate, compared with 9.78% for the 575.00M-parameter PhoneticXEUS baseline. A secondary child speech reading analysis shows a complementary operating profile: BranchShine is more conservative on incorrect readings, while Whisper-Medium is stronger on exact acceptance of correct readings. Overall, the results indicate that a compact raw-audio-to-IPA model can approach much larger baselines on character-level IPA transcription.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BranchShine is a compact 33M-parameter raw-audio CTC recognizer for IPA transcription, achieving 9.19% error rate on 41 languages, competitive with a 575M baseline.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出BranchShine，一个仅33M参数的轻量级原始音频到IPA转录模型，使用RoPE E-Branchformer编码器。在覆盖41种语言的多语言测试集上，其字符错误率为9.19%，与575M参数的PhoneticXEUS基线相当。该工作展示了紧凑模型在多语言语音转录中的潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出BranchShine，一个仅33M参数的轻量级原始音频到IPA转录模型，使用RoPE E-Branchformer编码器。在覆盖41种语言的多语言测试集上，其字符错误率为9.19%，与575M参数的PhoneticXEUS基线相当。该工作展示了紧凑模型在多语言语音转录中的潜力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: automatic speech recognition for multiple languages

quick_skim.title: Bridging Self-Supervised Learning and Speech Enhancement: A Wav2Vec2-Conditioned Framework
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion models show potential for speech enhancement but lack linguistic guidance. We condition a diffusion-based model on wav2vec 2.0 features from noisy input, injected at the U-Net bottleneck via Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM). Phonetic representations from wav2vec 2.0 features of degraded speech, anchor the reverse diffusion process. While a frozen wav2vec 2.0 encoder extracts features, a learned FiLM generator produces scale and shift parameters modulating the bottleneck with minimal overhead. Motivated by the optimal Bayesian causal estimator under a linear-Gaussian state-space model, FiLM coefficients are aggregated via exponential smoothing for temporal compression. Evaluation on VoiceBank-DEMAND and LibriMix shows competitive performance against the unconditioned baseline in PESQ, STOI, SI-SDR and DNSMOS. We consistently record an improvement of 0.4 on PESQ score, suggesting self-supervised representations effectively condition diffusion-based speech enhancement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diffusion-based speech enhancement conditioned on wav2vec 2.0 features
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散模型的语音增强，以wav2vec 2.0特征为条件
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于扩散模型的语音增强，以wav2vec 2.0特征为条件
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散模型的语音增强，以wav2vec 2.0特征为条件
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Conditions diffusion speech enhancement on wav2vec 2.0 features via FiLM, leveraging self-supervised representations to guide reverse diffusion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型在语音增强中缺乏语言指导。该文提出将wav2vec 2.0特征通过FiLM注入U-Net瓶颈，以噪声语音的声学特征引导逆向扩散过程。在VoiceBank-DEMAND和LibriMix上评估，证明该方法能有效利用自监督表示提升增强质量，且额外开销极小。

quick_skim.title: MSU-Bench: Towards Speaker-Centric Understanding in Conversational Multi-Speaker Scenarios
quick_skim.abstract: Spoken Language Understanding (SLU) is moving from task-specific pipelines toward large audio language models (LALMs) that generate natural-language responses. However, existing speech benchmarks mainly focus on single-speaker settings or isolated subtasks, leaving speaker-centric understanding in realistic multi-speaker conversations insufficiently evaluated. We introduce MSU-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark for multi-speaker conversational understanding, covering 16 speaker-centric tasks and 2,300 QA instances in a two-tier framework from speaker grounding to dialogue reasoning. We build a Gemini-assisted annotation and QA generation pipeline with human-in-the-loop verification, achieving high QA validity and strong agreement between human answers and verified labels. We further analyze speaker-referencing schemes and diagnostic error types to reveal bottlenecks in speaker grounding and reasoning. Experiments reveal clear gaps across model families, with closed-source systems leading overall but all models still facing challenges in complex speaker grounding and multi-speaker reasoning. The benchmark annotations, metadata, and evaluation scripts will be available at the GitHub repository: https://github.com/ASLP-lab/MSU-Bench.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-speaker conversational benchmark for audio language models, relevant to multilingual speech understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多说话人对话理解基准，适用于评估多语言语音系统
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多说话人对话理解基准，适用于评估多语言语音系统
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多说话人对话理解基准，适用于评估多语言语音系统
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MSU-Bench is a diagnostic benchmark for multi-speaker conversational understanding with 16 speaker-centric tasks, evaluating large audio language models in realistic scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有语音基准主要关注单说话人设置，缺乏对真实多说话人对话中说话人中心理解的评估。本文提出MSU-Bench，一个覆盖16个说话人中心任务和2300个问答实例的诊断基准，构建了基于Gemini的标注和问答生成pipeline。实验表明该基准能够有效评估大型音频语言模型的多说话人理解能力，对推动多语言语音技术发展具有重要价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有语音基准主要关注单说话人设置，缺乏对真实多说话人对话中说话人中心理解的评估。本文提出MSU-Bench，一个覆盖16个说话人中心任务和2300个问答实例的诊断基准，构建了基于Gemini的标注和问答生成pipeline。实验表明该基准能够有效评估大型音频语言模型的多说话人理解能力，对推动多语言语音技术发展具有重要价值。

quick_skim.title: From Sounds to Scenes: A Benchmark for Evaluating Context-Aware Auditory Scene Understanding in Large Audio Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Recent Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) have achieved remarkable progress in audio perceptual tasks across individual acoustic layers, including speech, sound, and music. However, existing benchmarks predominantly evaluate these layers in isolation, overlooking the complex contextual relationships that arise when multiple acoustic sources co-occur in real-world auditory scenes. Real-world auditory interpretation requires Context-Aware Auditory Scene Understanding (CASU): the ability to comprehend the holistic scene by integrating sound layers. To evaluate this capability, we introduce the CASU benchmark, which assesses whether Audio LLMs can interpret auditory scenes composed of speech, acoustic events (e.g., announcements), and background environments (e.g., traffic), and reason about the logical relationships between these layers. We propose a scalable pipeline for constructing time-accurate, semi-synthetic audio streams by composing real-world scene sounds with synthetic speech. Building on this data, we design four tasks that probe scene understanding: contextual question answering, entity extraction from the scene, speaker role inference, and counterfactual reasoning where scene is manipulated. Experiments across multiple LALMs demonstrate that effective auditory scene understanding requires integration over all auditory layers, rather than reliance on speech or sound alone, underscoring the necessity of CASU for advancing complex audio understanding in LALMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for context-aware auditory scene understanding, including speech, relevant to multilingual speech technologies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 上下文感知听觉场景理解基准，包含语音，与多语言语音技术相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 上下文感知听觉场景理解基准，包含语音，与多语言语音技术相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 上下文感知听觉场景理解基准，包含语音，与多语言语音技术相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CASU benchmark evaluates large audio language models on context-aware auditory scene understanding by integrating speech, sound, and music layers in realistic scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有音频评估通常孤立考虑不同声学层，缺乏对真实场景中复杂上下文关系的考察。本文提出CASU基准，用于评估大型音频语言模型在整合语音、声音和音乐等多声源场景中的理解能力。该基准为多语言语音技术提供了更全面的评估视角，有助于提升模型在复杂声学环境下的表现。

quick_skim.title: Synthesizing the Lombard Effect: Multi-Level Control of Speech Clarity and Vocal Effort in TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Humans tend to speak louder and clearer in challenging environments, such as noisy conditions or when addressing hearingimpaired listeners, which is called Lombard effect. To simulate this behavior in speech synthesis systems, we introduce a flow-matching based text-to-speech (TTS) model trained with vocal effort and articulation pseudo-labels. The proposed model achieves continuous and disentangled control of vocal effort and articulation, while also enabling word-level emphasis for clarifying specific segments of an utterance. Experimental results show that these control mechanisms effectively improve clarityrelated acoustic features. Furthermore, speech-in-noise experiments demonstrate that our model successfully simulates the intelligibility gains of human clear speech in noisy conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: controllable TTS with multi-level vocal effort and articulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多层级控制语音清晰度和发声努力的可控TTS
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多层级控制语音清晰度和发声努力的可控TTS
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多层级控制语音清晰度和发声努力的可控TTS
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a flow-matching TTS model with continuous and disentangled control of vocal effort and articulation, enabling word-level emphasis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为模拟嘈杂环境下说话更清晰响亮（Lombard效应），本文提出基于流匹配的TTS模型，通过训练发声努力和清晰度的伪标签实现连续解耦控制，并支持词级强调。实验表明该方法有效改善了清晰度相关声学特征，并在语音中成功模拟了Lombard效应。该工作为语种、语速、时长可控的TTS研究提供了多层级控制的实现思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 为模拟嘈杂环境下说话更清晰响亮（Lombard效应），本文提出基于流匹配的TTS模型，通过训练发声努力和清晰度的伪标签实现连续解耦控制，并支持词级强调。实验表明该方法有效改善了清晰度相关声学特征，并在语音中成功模拟了Lombard效应。该工作为语种、语速、时长可控的TTS研究提供了多层级控制的实现思路。

quick_skim.title: STEB: A Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark for Evaluating Beyond Translation Fidelity
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) should preserve not only lexical meaning, but also expressive attributes: emotion, scenario style (e.g., news reporting vs. dramatic dialogue), and nonverbal vocalizations (NVs). Moreover, collecting cross-lingual target speech that is both translation-faithful and expressively aligned with the source is difficult at scale, making reference-based evaluation impractical. We introduce STEB (Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark), a 32.6-hour Chinese--English benchmark that evaluates both standard dimensions (translation fidelity, speaker similarity, duration alignment) and expressiveness dimensions (emotion, scenario style, NV preservation). For expressiveness evaluation, STEB uses a caption-then-summarize framework that converts speech into structured expressive attributes and compares source and hypothesis attributes with an LLM judge. Human validation shows statistically significant correlations with listener judgments across all expressive dimensions. We evaluate six S2ST systems covering cascaded systems, end-to-end models, and speech large language models. Many systems, especially cascaded ones, achieve strong translation fidelity, but they still struggle with emotion preservation (best: 3.82/5) and NV preservation (best: 2.31/5). These results reveal a gap between semantic transfer and expressive transfer, identifying expressiveness preservation as an open challenge for S2ST. Audio samples are available at https://cmots.github.io/steb.github.io/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音到语音翻译表达力基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音到语音翻译表达力基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音到语音翻译表达力基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces STEB, a 32.6-hour Chinese-English speech-to-speech translation benchmark evaluating both translation fidelity and expressive dimensions like emotion and scenario style.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出STEB，一个32.6小时的中英语音到语音翻译表达力基准。除传统翻译保真度外，还评估情感、场景风格和非语言发声等表达维度，为难于收集参考数据的跨语言表达力评估提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: FlowTTS-GRPO: Online Reinforcement Learning with Multi-Objective Reward Optimization for Flow-Matching Based Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: Existing Reinforcement Learning (RL) research for Text-to-Speech (TTS) focuses on large language models (LLMs), leaving Flow-Matching (FM) under-explored. We present FlowTTS-GRPO, an online RL framework for FM-based TTS. By converting ordinary differential equation (ODE) trajectories into stochastic differential equation (SDE) paths, our method enables direct fine-tuning of open-source FM models without auxiliary models. We show that a weighted reward combination converges faster than a probabilistic scheme, and identify three practical optimizations: omitting classifier-free guidance (CFG) during training accelerates convergence; synthesizing hard cases improves robustness; and applying RL to the FM component enhances audio-detail metrics. Experiments on CosyVoice 3.0 and F5-TTS demonstrate objective and subjective preference gains in speaker similarity and perceptual quality, with F5-TTS also improving intelligibility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Flow-matching TTS with online reinforcement learning optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于流匹配的文本转语音在线强化学习方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于流匹配的文本转语音在线强化学习方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于流匹配的文本转语音在线强化学习方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FlowTTS-GRPO, an online RL framework for flow-matching based TTS that converts ODE trajectories to SDE paths for direct fine-tuning, showing weighted reward combination converges faster and identifies practical optimizations like omitting CFG and synthesizing hard cases.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的文本转语音(TTS)强化学习研究主要聚焦于大语言模型，忽略了流匹配模型。为此，本文提出FlowTTS-GRPO，一个面向流匹配TTS的在线强化学习框架。该方法将常微分方程轨迹转换为随机微分方程路径，无需辅助模型即可直接微调开源流匹配模型。实验表明，加权奖励组合比概率方案收敛更快，并总结出三项实用优化：训练时省略无分类器引导可加速收敛、合成难例增强鲁棒性、对流匹配模型应用强化学习有效。该工作填补了流匹配TTS的RL空白，为TTS优化提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: One Model, Many Latencies: Universal Speech Enhancement for Diverse Real-Time Applications
quick_skim.abstract: Different real-time speech applications impose distinct latency budgets, often requiring separately trained enhancement models for each scenario. In this paper, we propose a one-for-all, real-time universal speech enhancement model that provides explicit control over both algorithmic and computational latency. Algorithmic latency is flexibly adjusted via configurable look-ahead frames. To avoid learning inefficiency caused by varying padding configurations, we introduce parallel convolutional layers corresponding to different look-ahead settings. Computational latency is controlled through an early-exit mechanism, enabling inference at different network depths. To narrow the performance gap between specialized and flexible models, we propose a two-stage training strategy with a shared-to-multiple decoder transition. Overall, the proposed framework enables a single model to be deployed across diverse latency budgets without retraining separate models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: universal speech enhancement model with latency control for diverse applications
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用语音增强模型，支持多种实时应用的延迟控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用语音增强模型，支持多种实时应用的延迟控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用语音增强模型，支持多种实时应用的延迟控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a one-for-all speech enhancement model with configurable algorithmic and computational latency via parallel convolutional layers and early-exit mechanism.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有实时语音应用需要针对不同延迟预算单独训练增强模型。该文提出一个通用实时语音增强模型，通过并行卷积层实现可配置的算法延迟，并通过早期退出机制控制计算延迟。在多个数据集上的实验表明，该模型在灵活性和性能之间取得了良好平衡，为多场景语音增强提供了统一方案。

quick_skim.title: ParaPairAudioBench: Paralinguistic Pairwise Audio Benchmark for LALM-as-a-Judge
quick_skim.abstract: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) have been widely used as judge models for the automatic evaluation of generated speech. However, prior approaches predominantly focus on holistic naturalness, leaving fine-grained paralinguistic distinctions underexplored. We introduce ParaPairAudioBench, a pairwise benchmark of 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions: Style, Rate, Emphasis, Age, and Gender. Our experiments show that current LALM judges still lag behind human judgments by 32%p on average and exhibit severe calibration failures, particularly in Tie cases where the correct decision is to abstain. To further analyze lexical versus acoustic reliance, the benchmark includes both same-transcript and cross-transcript conditions. ParaPairAudioBench enables multi-dimensional, calibration-aware assessment of the reliability of LALM-as-a-Judge for paralinguistic speech evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: paralinguistic pairwise audio benchmark for evaluating LALM judges across dimensions like rate and style
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向语音评估的副语言学配对音频基准，涵盖速率和风格等维度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向语音评估的副语言学配对音频基准，涵盖速率和风格等维度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向语音评估的副语言学配对音频基准，涵盖速率和风格等维度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ParaPairAudioBench, a pairwise benchmark for evaluating LALMs as judges on paralinguistic dimensions (style, rate, emphasis, age, gender), revealing significant gaps vs humans.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出了ParaPairAudioBench，一个包含5175个音频对的配对基准，用于评估大型音频语言模型在风格、速率、强调、年龄、性别等副语言学维度上的判断能力。实验表明当前模型平均落后人类32%个百分点，且在平局情况下严重校准失败。该基准包含相同文本和跨文本条件，有助于分析词汇与声学线索的依赖。这项工作为生成语音的细粒度自动评估提供了重要工具。

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Oscillatory Inductive Bias for Modeling Sharp Prosodic Dynamics in Diffusion-Based TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based text-to-speech (TTS) models have achieved significant improvements in speech quality. However, modeling sharp prosodic transitions and rapid pitch variations in expressive speech remains challenging. Existing diffusion-based TTS decoders commonly utilize periodic nonlinearities such as Snake activation function to capture harmonic structures, but this activation funcation provides limited adaptability when modeling abrupt amplitude and frequency variations. In this paper, we investigate the role of oscillatory inductive bias in diffusion-based TTS decoders and introduce an adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity that enables controllable periodic modulation while maintaining signal stability through a linear bypass component. We refer the resulting TTS system as OscillaTTS. Experiments on the LJSpeech and Emotional Speech Dataset show consistent improvements across objective and subjective evaluations, indicating improved modeling of expressive prosodic dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptive oscillatory bias for modeling prosody in diffusion-based TTS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自适应振荡偏置用于扩散文本转语音中的韵律建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自适应振荡偏置用于扩散文本转语音中的韵律建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自适应振荡偏置用于扩散文本转语音中的韵律建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity in diffusion-based TTS decoders to better model sharp prosodic dynamics and rapid pitch variations, improving expressive speech quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对扩散文本转语音模型在表达性语音中难以模拟急剧韵律变化的问题，提出一种自适应振荡非线性激活函数。该函数在保持信号稳定性的同时，通过线性旁路实现可控的周期性调制，从而更精确地捕捉快速音高变化。实验表明该方法显著提升了自然度和表现力，为TTS中的韵律建模提供了新的归纳偏置。

quick_skim.title: Fully Differentiable Neural Forced Alignment via Soft Dynamic Programming
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in sequence modeling have significantly improved ASR systems, bringing them close to human-level recognition accuracy and enhancing robustness across diverse acoustic conditions and languages. In contrast, Forced Alignment has not experienced comparable progress, and traditional HMM-GMM frameworks remain widely adopted and highly competitive.   To address this gap, we propose an end-to-end, fully differentiable neural architecture specifically designed for phoneme alignment. The model consists of an encoder that processes the input signal and a decoder that produces alignment decisions. The encoder is structured into two complementary branches: one dedicated to phoneme identity verification and the other to phoneme boundary detection. The decoder is implemented as a trainable module based on differentiable soft dynamic programming. The entire system is optimized end-to-end using a novel contrastive loss that encourages clear separation between steady-state phoneme regions and transition boundaries.   The proposed approach outperforms the current state of the art in phoneme alignment on hand-annotated English benchmarks, achieves strong word-level generalization results, and demonstrates generalization on unseen languages.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: fully differentiable neural forced alignment for phoneme alignment, improving ASR robustness across languages
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全微分神经强制对齐，提升多语言ASR鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 全微分神经强制对齐，提升多语言ASR鲁棒性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 全微分神经强制对齐，提升多语言ASR鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an end-to-end differentiable neural architecture for phoneme alignment with two encoder branches, improving forced alignment over traditional HMM-GMM frameworks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文提出一种端到端全微分神经强制对齐架构，编码器包含音素身份验证和边界检测两个互补分支，直接生成音素对齐。克服了传统HMM-GMM方法的局限，并增强了跨语言和声学条件的鲁棒性。对多语言语音识别系统的基础对齐组件有重要改进意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该文提出一种端到端全微分神经强制对齐架构，编码器包含音素身份验证和边界检测两个互补分支，直接生成音素对齐。克服了传统HMM-GMM方法的局限，并增强了跨语言和声学条件的鲁棒性。对多语言语音识别系统的基础对齐组件有重要改进意义。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: automatic speech recognition for multiple languages

quick_skim.title: RedVox: Safety and Fairness Gaps in Speech Models Across Languages
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-capable models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications across languages. Yet their safety and fairness beyond English settings and under naturalistic conditions remain understudied. We survey safety reporting practices across state-of-the-art speech model releases, finding that only 8% document any multilingual analysis. To address this gap, we introduce RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for audio and speech built on real voices, covering unsafe and unfair stereotypical requests across five languages (English, French, Italian, Spanish, and German). Evaluating eight state-of-the-art models, we find that vulnerabilities persist even under non-adversarial conditions, worsen in non-English languages, and are amplified when the request comes from a spoken input. Finally, by surveying the participants who contributed to RedVox, we document the unique personal and privacy challenges of collecting speech data with human participants, pointing to broader sociotechnical challenges in naturalistic speech safety research.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for speech models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多语言语音模型的安全性与公平性基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多语言语音模型的安全性与公平性基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多语言语音模型的安全性与公平性基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RedVox introduces a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for speech models across five languages, finding that only 8% of model releases document multilingual analysis and that vulnerabilities worsen in non-English languages.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前语音模型的安全性和公平性评估多集中于英语，缺乏多语言环境下的系统研究。本文调研发现仅有8%的模型发布文档包含多语言分析。为此，作者构建了RedVox，一个覆盖五种语言（英语、法语、意大利语、西班牙语和德语）的多语言语音安全与公平基准。对八个先进模型的评估显示，安全漏洞在非对抗条件下依然存在，在非英语语言中更加严重，并呈现放大效应。该基准填补了多语言语音模型安全评估的空白，为后续研究提供了重要工具。
